taxonomy: Balaniella abbreviata is classified as a species of bryozoan, belonging to the phylum Bryozoa. It is a colonial, sessile animal that filters food particles from the water. The phylum Bryozoa consists of over 5,000 described species, and Balaniella abbreviata is just one of them. Bryozoans are aquatic animals that are often found in shallow marine environments. Their classification is further divided into several classes, including Stenolaemata, Phylactolaemata, and Gymnolaemata.
uses: Balaniella abbreviata has been reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is used for its anthelmintic properties, to treat parasitic worm infections. The plant's roots are also used in folk medicine to treat fever and other related conditions.
